q:
Pumpkin Weights
A botanist is studying the weights, measured in pounds, of pumpkins grown at two different farms. The boxplots show the distribution of pumpkin weight for two samples of equal size, one from farm A and the other from farm B. Based on the boxplots, which of the following is a correct conclusion about the distribution of pumpkin weight for the samples from the two farms?
o: The interquartile ranges of the pumpkin weights are the same for farm A and farm B.
The range of the pumpkin weights for farm B is greater than the range for farm A.
There are more pumpkin weights that are greater than 18 pounds from farm A than there are from farm B.
There are more pumpkin weights that are greater than 12 pounds from farm B than there are from farm A.
There are more pumpkin weights that are less than 10 pounds from farm B than there are from farm A.

q:
Body Temperature
The distribution of body temperature for healthy adults is approximately normal with mean 98.2°F and standard deviation 0.7°F. Based on the description of the distribution, which of the following statements CANNOT be supported?
o: More than 95% of healthy adults have a body temperature greater than 96.8 degrees Fahrenheit .
The first quartile of the distribution of healthy adults’ body temperature is less than 97.5 degrees Fahrenheit .
Approximately 50% of healthy adults have a body temperature greater than 98.2 degrees Fahrenheit .
Approximately 95% of healthy adults have a body temperature between 96.8 degrees Fahrenheit and 99.6 degrees Fahrenheit .
A body temperature of 100.3 degrees Fahrenheit or more would occur in less than 2% of healthy adults.

q:
Striped Bass
Gwyneth measured the weights, in pounds, of ten striped bass taken from a local river. She calculated a mean weight of 13 pounds and a range of 5 pounds. For a report, Gwyneth converted the measurements from pounds to kilograms. (Note: There is approximately 0.454 kilogram in 1 pound.)Which of the following is closest to the mean and range of the converted weights?
o: The mean weight is 28.63 kilograms, and the range is 5 kilograms.
The mean weight is 28.63 kilograms, and the range is 2.27 kilograms.
The mean weight is 5.90 kilograms, and the range is 11.01 kilograms.
The mean weight is 5.90 kilograms, and the range is 5 kilograms.
The mean weight is 5.90 kilograms, and the range is 2.27 kilograms.

q:
Cranberries
A grocery store received three shipments of bags of cranberries: one from farm A, one from farm B, and one from farm C. The distributions of the weight of a bag are unimodal and symmetric for all three shipments. The number of bags and the mean and standard deviation, in ounces (oz), for each shipment are shown in the table. Farm Number of Bags Mean (oz) Standard Deviation (oz) A 100 16.0 0.5 B 100 16.0 0.1 C 100 16.0 0.1 Which of the following statements is true?
o: The shipment from farm A is most likely to have the greatest number of bags that weigh less than 16 ounces .
The shipment from farm B is most likely to have the greatest number of bags that weigh less than 16 ounces .
The shipment from farm C is most likely to have the greatest number of bags that weigh less than 16 ounces .
The shipments from farms B and C are the only shipments that are likely to have an approximately equal number of bags that weigh less than 16 ounces .
The shipments from farms A, B, and C are all likely to have an approximately equal number of bags that weigh less than 16 ounces .

q:
Long Jump Length
The distribution of long jump distance in a statewide high school league is approximately normal with a mean of 18.5 feet and a standard deviation of 3.2 feet. Two students, Joshua and Mateo, from this league competed in the long jump. Joshua’s distance was 0.52 standard deviation below the mean of the distribution. Mateo’s distance was at the 48th percentile of the distribution.At approximately what percentile was the distance jumped by Joshua, and how did his distance compare to Mateo’s distance?
o: Joshua’s distance was at the 30th percentile of the distribution and was less than Mateo’s distance.
Joshua’s distance was at the 30th percentile of the distribution and was greater than Mateo’s distance.
Joshua’s distance was at the 48th percentile of the distribution and was less than Mateo’s distance.
Joshua’s distance was at the 48th percentile of the distribution and was greater than Mateo’s distance.
Joshua’s distance was at the 70th percentile of the distribution and was greater than Mateo’s distance.

The segmented bar chart shows the distribution for each retail outlet for the three jewelry types based on sales data for the past week. Based on the chart, which statement must be true?
o: The percent of earring sales at department stores was less than the percent of necklace sales at specialty stores.
For each retail outlet category, less than 30% of sales were earrings.
The number of earring sales for department stores was the same as the number of earring sales for warehouse clubs.
The greatest number of ring sales came from warehouse clubs.
The number of ring sales for specialty stores was greater than the number of ring sales for warehouse clubs.

q:
Coffee shop employees
Suppose a new regulation requires businesses to pay all employees a minimum wage of $15.00 per hour. A small coffee shop currently has 9 employees—5 employees whose wages are less than $15.00 per hour and 4 employees whose wages are more than $15.00 per hour. To meet the new regulation, the coffee shop plans to increase the wages of the 5 employees whose wages are less than $15.00 per hour to $15.00 per hour. The other 4 employees will not receive a wage increase at this time. Which of the following must be true about the median wage of the 9 employees after the wage increase occurs?
o: The median wage will be greater than $15.00 per hour.
The median wage will increase but will be less than $15.00 per hour.
The median wage will be $15.00 per hour.
The median wage will not change.
The median wage cannot be determined from the information provided.

q:
Standardized scores for Iowa and Ohio corn weight
A grocery store receives deliveries of corn from two farms, one in Iowa and the other in Ohio. Both farms produce ears of corn with mean weight 1.26 pounds. The standard deviation of the weights of the ears of corn from the farm in Ohio is 0.01 pound greater than that from the farm in Iowa. A randomly selected ear of corn from the farm in Iowa weighed 1.39 pounds, which has a standardized score of 1.645 for the distribution of weights for the Iowa corn. If an ear of corn from the farm in Ohio weighs 1.39 pounds, how many standard deviations from the mean is the weight with respect to the Ohio distribution?
o: 1.46 standard deviations below the mean
1.46 standard deviations above the mean
1.65 standard deviations above the mean
1.88 standard deviations below the mean
1.88 standard deviations above the mean

Age-Group (years) Minimum Q 1 Median Q 3 Maximum 20 to 30 60 71 72 75 84 40 to 50 60 70 73 76 85 The values 60 , 62 , and 84 were common to both samples. The three values are identified as outliers with respect to the age-group 20 years to 30 years because they are either 1.5 times the interquartile range ( I Q R ) greater than the upper quartile or 1.5 times the I Q R less than the lower quartile. Using the same method for identifying outliers, which of the three values are identified as outliers for the age-group 40 years to 50 years?
o: None of the three values is identified as an outlier.
Only 60 is identified as an outlier.
Only 60 and 62 are identified as outliers.
Only 60 and 84 are identified as outliers.
The three values are all identified as outliers.

Paper Helicopters

A histogram of the flight times, in seconds, and a scatterplot of flight time versus blade width, in centimeters (cm), are shown for 25 helicopters. The left cluster displays flight times for blade widths of 1.5 centimeters , and the right cluster displays flight times for the remaining blade widths.
The left cluster displays minimum flight times for each of the blade widths, and the right cluster displays the remaining flight times.
The clusters are not related to the blade widths because flight time is measured in seconds and blade widths are measured in centimeters.
The clusters are not related to the blade widths because the relationship between blade width and flight time is not linear.

Mode
Range

The median drop-out rate of the 49 high schools decreased between the 1989-90 and 1992-1993 school years.

Research indicates that the standard deviation of typical human body temperature is 0.4 degree Celsius (C). Which of the following represents the standard deviation of typical human body temperature in degrees Fahrenheit (F), where F = 9 5 C + 32 ?
o: 9 fifths times 0 point 4 plus 32
9 fifths times 0 point 4
9 fifths times 0 point 4 squared
9 fifths squared times 0 point 4
9 fifths squared times 0 point 4 squared

q:
Normal proportion for female runner weights
The distribution of weights of female college cross-country runners is approximately normal with mean 122 pounds and standard deviation 8 pounds. Which of the following is closest to the percent of the runners who weigh between 114 pounds and 138 pounds?

Based on the dotplots, which of the following is greater for the percent moisture of corn than for the percent moisture of soybeans?

q:
Compute mean sleep hours for newborn babies
A sleep time of 15.9 hours per day for a newborn baby is at the 10th percentile of the distribution of sleep times for all newborn babies. Assuming the distribution is normal with standard deviation 0.5 hour, approximately what is the mean sleep time, in hours per day, for newborn babies?

Remove outlier for temperature measurements
As part of a science experiment, a student recorded 10 measurements of the temperature of a liquid. One of the measurements was an outlier when compared with the other 9 measurements. (Note: An outlier is any number that is greater than the upper quartile or less than the lower quartile by at least 1.5 times the interquartile range.) Which of the following must be true about the 9 measurements, excluding the outlier, when compared with the 10 measurements?
o: The median of the 9 measurements is less than the median of the 10 measurements.
The median of the 9 measurements is greater than the median of the 10 measurements.
The maximum of the 9 measurements is less than the maximum of the 10 measurements.
The maximum of the 9 measurements is greater than the maximum of the 10 measurements.
The standard deviation of the 9 measurements is less than the standard deviation of the 10 measurements.

q:
Normal proportion for large egg carton weights
At a certain store, the distribution of weights of cartons of large eggs is approximately normal with mean 26 ounces (oz). Based on the distribution, which of the following intervals will contain the greatest proportion of cartons of large eggs at the store?
o: 20 oz to 24 oz
22 oz to 26 oz
24 oz to 28 oz
26 oz to 30 oz
28 oz to 32 oz

q:
Compare boxplots of golf tournament scores
In a standard golf tournament, golfers play 18 holes of golf on each of 4 consecutive days. For each hole, golfers keep track of the number of times they hit the ball (strokes) before the ball goes into the cup. A golfer’s score for the tournament is the total number of strokes needed to complete the tournament. The boxplots below summarize the scores for golfers who competed in tournament 1 and golfers who competed in tournament 2. Based on the boxplots, which of the following statements must be true?
o: More golfers played in tournament 1 than in tournament 2.
In both tournaments, at least half the golfers completed the tournament with a score less than 288.
The number of golfers who completed tournament 1 with a score less than 288 was greater than the number of golfers who completed tournament 2 with a score less than 288.
The range of scores for tournament 1 is less than the range of scores for tournament 2.
The score of the golfer with the least score in tournament 1 was greater than the score of the golfer with the least score in tournament 2.

q:
Standardized score for Canadian and U.S. dollars
Nyasha’s financial literacy project involved comparing the annual sales of companies in Canada and companies in the United States that produce software. Using the ratio of 1 Canadian dollar to 0.75 United States dollar, she converted all annual sales from the Canadian companies into United States dollars. For which of the following will the value of the statistic for the annual sales in Canadian dollars be equal to the value of the corresponding statistic in United States dollars?

The standard deviation of the annual sales
The standardized score of the minimum annual sales
The mean annual sales
The interquartile range of the annual sales

q:

o: 250 yards
300 yards
350 yards
400 yards
450 yards

q:
Shape of distribution of well-prepared employees
New employees at a large corporation go through a training program during their first week of employment. The new employees take a written assessment at the completion of the program to determine how well prepared they are for their jobs. A score greater than the mean indicates a well-prepared employee. Assume the following distributions of new employee scores have the same mean score, the same maximum score, and the same minimum score. Which distribution has a shape that is most likely to represent the greatest percent of well-prepared employees?
o: The distribution of scores is skewed to the right.
The distribution of scores is skewed to the left.
The distribution of scores is bimodal and symmetric.
The distribution of scores is uniform.
The distribution of scores is approximately normal.

q:
Normal proportion for smartphone assembly times
The distribution of assembly times required to assemble a certain smartphone is approximately normal with mean 4.6 minutes and standard deviation 0.6 minute. Of the following, which is closest to the percentage of assembly times between 4 minutes and 5 minutes?

Normal probability for mean lake water level
Scientists working for a water district measure the water level in a lake each day. The daily water level in the lake varies due to weather conditions and other factors. The daily water level has a distribution that is approximately normal with mean water level of 84.07 feet. The probability that the daily water level in the lake is at least 100 feet is 0.064. Which of the following is closest to the probability that on a randomly selected day the water level in the lake will be at least 90 feet?

q:
Favorite subject
Students in a class were asked to choose their favorite school subject. The following frequency table summarizes the responses. School Subject Frequency Math 7 English 5 Science 4 Social Studies 2 Other 2 Which of the following statements is supported by the frequency table?

Seventy percent of the students chose math as their favorite subject.
Less than half of the students chose math as their favorite subject.

q:
Favorite color
A group of first-grade students were asked to name their favorite color. The responses are shown in the following frequency table.Color Frequency Red 15 Blue 30 Green 8 Yellow 12 Purple 25 Which of the following statements is supported by the frequency table?

Twice as many students chose blue as chose red.
Yellow was chosen by the least number of students.
The total number of student responses is 80.
The combined number of students who chose green and yellow is greater than the number of students who chose purple.

From 100 square feet up to but not including 150 square feet, 3. From 150 square feet up to but not including 200 square feet, 6. From 200 square feet up to but not including 250 square feet, 1. From 250 square feet up to but not including 300 square feet, 7. From 300 square feet up to but not including 350 square feet, 3.
The figure presents a 3 by 13 grid. The horizontal axis is labeled “Room Size, in square feet,” and the numbers 100 through 360, in increments of 20, are indicated.
